meta.title: Kitami Wakamatsu Municipal Ski Area
meta.description: Fifteen minutes from downtown Kitami—twice-daily groomed burns, city night views, and municipal night skiing until 21:00.

hero.title:
Kitami Wakamatsu
Municipal Ski Area
hero.tagline: Fifteen minutes from the city—after work or after school on a municipal hill of groomed burns and night views.

live.subtitle: Transparent info from Kitami City operations

lift.label: Lifts
lift.note: 1 pair lift · 1 rope tow · virtually no wait

courses.label: Courses
courses.note: Up to 1,080 m · SAJ-certified course B

night.label: Night skiing
night.note: From 16:00 · weekday evenings are the core

transit.title:
Fifteen minutes from town,
on the hill after work.
transit.lead: About 15–20 minutes by car from JR Kitami Station on the Sekihoku Line—a city-adjacent municipal hill at the heart of the Okhotsk region. A local hill built around weekday night skiing, welcoming Memanbetsu Airport arrivals and Kitami residents' after-work and after-school laps alike.
transit.imgAlt: Fifteen minutes from downtown Kitami—access to an urban municipal ski hill

primary.title: Twice-daily grooming & city night views
primary.lead: Snowmaking plus morning and evening grooming keep a tight, edge-gripping burn—from the 1,080 m A-course long run to SAJ-certified B-course for racers. Kitami's city lights spread below the summit: a signature view of urban night skiing.
primary.imgAlt: Kitami Wakamatsu Municipal Ski Area—groomed burns with Kitami city below

secondary.title: Family hill & kids' sled area
secondary.lead: A mellow family slope averaging 10° and a dedicated kids' sled zone—the municipal gateway for Kitami families, school clubs, and ski school practice on an everyday, high-quality local hill.

pass.title:
Adults from ¥3,000,
1-hour night from ¥700.
pass.lead: Municipal pricing for local repeaters and weekday nights—adult day ¥3,000–3,900, 4-hour ¥2,200–2,800, 1-hour night ¥700–1,000, season pass ¥33,000. Designed for short, focused sessions and higher visit frequency (LP concept).
pass.noticeBody: Weekday night monthly passes and time-based digital tickets to offset cold-weather short visits and perceived value gaps—raising frequency and LTV (report ch. 6).
pass.imgAlt: Fifteen minutes from Kitami Station—city proximity as the weekday night lever

rental.title:
Gear up at the ski center—
travel light.
rental.lead: Full ski and snowboard sets at the ski center—Memanbetsu Airport visitors can gear up on site and start runs immediately. Counter rental, no phone reservation required.
rental.whisper: Hands-free arrival plus a 15-minute city location supports international "urban night ski experience" packages (LP strategy).
rental.imgAlt: Ski center rental counter—hands-free arrival concept

yukinko.title:
Night skiing
until 21:00.
yukinko.lead: Fully lit night skiing to the summit (16:00–21:00)—after-work laps for locals, after-school runs for students, and first runs for late arrivals. Kitami's city lights below and twice-daily groomed burns anchor weekday evenings.
yukinko.whisper: In season TEL 0157-22-2910. Official updates also on Kitami City and Okhotsk Kikaku sites.
yukinko.imgAlt: Illuminated night-ski slopes—Kitami city lights in the background

family.title:
School practice hill &
first snow for families.
family.lead: Three courses plus a family hill, kids' sled zone, and rope-tow learner slope—the municipal gateway for Kitami school programs, clubs, and family winter sports.
family.noticeBody: Training base for local school clubs and ski schools—SAJ-certified B-course supports racers too. Nurturing the next generation of winter sports fans.
family.imgAlt: Family hill and kids' sled zone—first snow for families

workation.title:
After work, 15 minutes—
night ski, then yakiniku.
workation.lead: Fifteen minutes by car from Kitami—a 1-hour night ticket for focused carving, then downtown yakiniku before 21:00. A safe weekday micro-tourism loop with designated drivers and taxis (LP concept).

01.title: Head to Wakamatsu after work
01.body: About 15 minutes from downtown Kitami—close enough for a spontaneous after-work lap

02.title: 1-hour night ticket
02.body: Use the night window from 16:00 for a focused 1–1.5 hour session

03.title: Carve the groomed burn
03.body: Twice-daily grooming on B-course for carving practice

04.title: Ski center
04.body: Warm up at the base lodge after runs—essential in deep Okhotsk cold

05.title: Kitami yakiniku
05.body: After-ski at downtown favorites like Mikakuen or Hyakumanriki

local.title: City-adjacent municipal night ski guide
local.body: Core weekday-night strategy—make the physical assets visible via mobile LP and live status: 15 minutes from Kitami Station, night skiing until 21:00, twice-daily grooming (report ch. 5).

schools.title: Time-based subscriptions & digital tickets
schools.body: Weekday night monthly passes and 15-minute digital tickets—lower the psychological barrier for short visits and dramatically raise frequency (report ch. 6).

summer.title: Night ski & Kitami yakiniku loop
summer.body: A weekday evening model pairing groomed burns with Kitami yakiniku soul food—linked to onsen routes at Kitami Onsen and Shiobetsu Tsurutsuru Onsen (report ch. 5).

access.note: About 15–20 minutes by car from JR Kitami Station · ~40 min from Memanbetsu Airport · 500–700 free parking spaces
